rulings.Case Title: JAYANT AGARWAL VS. DEMETER GROUP HOLDINGS, LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 18, 2010, line 3. PLAINTIFF JAYANT AGARWAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. There is a triable issue of fact regarding the meaning of the 2009 Bonus terms. The terms of the contract are ambiguous and subject to conflicting parole evidence (plaintiff's supplemental declaration paragraph 4 and Meashe's declaration paragraph 4). See Wolf v. Superior Court (2004) 114 Cal App 4th 1343, 1350-1351. Defendant to prepare an order in compliance with C.C.P. section 437c(g). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CHASE BANK, USA, N.A. VS. ELISE A. CAMPEAU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 18, 2010, line 6. CROSS DEFENDANT CHASE BANK, USA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled, 10 days leave to answer. Cross-defendant's motion is based on the erroneous premise that the challenged causes of action were preempted by federal law. However, C.C. 1785.25(a) is not preempted, and no cause of action relies solely on provisions that are preempted. New theories may not be raised in the reply. Demurrer is not an appropriate vehicle to challenge part of a cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS WALKER et al VS. PLATINUM CAPITAL GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 18, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT AURORA LOAN SERVICES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED, 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND IF PLAINTIFFS CAN, IN GOOD FAITH, PLEAD TENDER OF THE AMOUNT OWED. CONSPIRACY IS NOT PLEAD WITH SPECIFICITY. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS WALKER et al VS. PLATINUM CAPITAL GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 18, 2010, line 8, Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction. DENIED, PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO SHOW A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. PLAINTIFFS ARE REQUIRED TO TENDER THE AMOUNT OWED BEFORE MAINTANING ANY CAUSE OF ACTION FOR IRREGULARITY IN THE SALE PROCEDURE. SEE ABDALLAH V. UNITED SAVINGS BANK (1996) 43 CAL. APP. 4TH 1101. A MORTGAGOR IS REQUIRED TO CLEAR HIS DEBT BEFORE HE CAN CLEAR TITLE. AGUILAR V. BOCCI (1974) 39 CAL. APP. 3d 475. NO FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS ASSERTED GIVE RISE TO A CONSPIRACY AMONG DEFENDANTS TO COMMIT TORTOUS ACTS AGAINST PLAINTIFF. UPON THE WRITTEN DEED OF TRUST, DEFENDANT WAS ALLOWED TO BEGIN NON-JUDICIAL FORECLOSURE PROCEEDINGS UPON PLAINTIFF'S DEFAULT. CC §2923.5 CHALLENGE TO THE SALE IS APPLICABLE BEFORE THE FORECLOSURE SALE. MABRY V. SUPERIOR COURT (2010) 185 CAL. APP. 4TH 208. =(302/CWW)
